message:
feat(charmed): add theme switching tests and cross-platform terminal integration tests

Theme Switching Tests (bd-2oku):
- Add comprehensive tests for theme preset switching invariants
- Verify all semantic tokens change when switching between dark/light/dracula
- Ensure all tokens are populated and valid hex colors for every preset
- Test that style helpers (title_style, success_style, badge_primary_style, etc.)
  produce different output per theme
- Verify card_style uses theme bg_subtle and panel_style uses theme border
- Add tests for semantic color helpers across all presets
- Test contrast enforcement for accessibility compliance

Cross-Platform Terminal Tests (bd-2zkx):
- Add integration tests for cross-platform terminal support in lipgloss
- Test color profile detection and graceful degradation
- Verify backend abstraction (AnsiBackend, HtmlBackend, PlainBackend)
- Test ANSI sequence stripping and detection utilities
- Cover adaptive colors, complete colors, and color profile handling
- Verify renderer configuration and dark background mode

These tests ensure consistent theming behavior across different terminal
environments and validate the theme system's ability to adapt colors
appropriately for various display conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2myg","title":"Dashboard: live metrics + status indicators","description":"Wire dashboard widgets to the demo data model so the dashboard is *alive* (real-time updates, plausible trends, state changes).

Why:
- Static numbers look fake. Live metrics make the dashboard feel like a real ops tool.
- This also validates the simulation engine and the app’s tick/update architecture.

Requirements (minimum):
- Live-updating metrics:
  - requests/sec (or throughput)
  - p95 latency (or similar)
  - error rate
  - job throughput / queue depth
- Health/status indicators:
  - per-metric health state (ok/warn/error) derived from thresholds
  - trends (up/down/flat) based on deltas over a window
  - at least one periodic “incident” / degradation to prove the UI can surface issues
- Feedback:
  - state transitions produce a toast/alert + (optional) log entry
  - drill-down panel should explain *why* a metric is warn/error (thresholds, recent values)

Implementation notes:
- Metrics come from the deterministic simulation engine; use a seed in test/E2E.
- Avoid noisy/flappy states:
  - consider hysteresis or smoothing so warn/error doesn’t flicker on boundaries.

Acceptance:
- Values visibly change over time.
- Health transitions are obvious and explainable.
- No flicker/jank; dashboard remains responsive.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - health state computation (thresholds + hysteresis if used)
  - trend computation over a known sequence
  - “incident” generation is deterministic for a fixed seed
- E2E:
  - smoke tour sees changing values
  - settings/theme scenario confirms dashboard updates immediately after toggles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.979377768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:38.344449577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:38.344380729Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:47:27.312380976Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.995055004Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:16.935347602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:18.142227251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw3","title":"lipgloss: Code Consolidation & ANSI Parsing Hardening","description":"# Epic: lipgloss Code Consolidation & ANSI Parsing Hardening

## Background
The lipgloss crate provides terminal styling (colors, borders, layout). During deep review,
code duplication and incomplete ANSI handling were identified as maintenance risks.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Three Independent visible_width() Implementations - HIGH
**Locations**:
- lib.rs lines 365-417: Full state machine with Normal, Esc, Csi, Osc states
- style.rs lines 1965-1996: Simplified two-state approach
- backend.rs lines 621-640: Even more simplified, no OSC handling

**Problem**: Each version has different completeness levels. They may produce different
results for the same input, causing layout inconsistencies.

**Risk**: Maintenance nightmare - bug fixes need to be applied to 3 places.

### 2. Incomplete OSC Sequence Handling - MEDIUM
**Location**: lib.rs lines 403-411
**Problem**: OSC sequence termination (ST = ESC \) is not properly validated.
The state machine transitions to Esc state on seeing ESC but doesn't verify
the following backslash character.

**Risk**: Malformed OSC sequences could cause incorrect width calculations.

### 3. Border Color Indexing Confusion - LOW
**Problem**: Border colors stored as [top, right, bottom, left] but accessed
inconsistently across different methods.

**Risk**: Wrong colors could be applied to wrong edges in edge cases.

## Design Philosophy
- Single Source of Truth: One canonical visible_width implementation
- Defensive Parsing: Handle all edge cases in ANSI parsing
- Clear Documentation: Document the expected format for all color arrays

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single visible_width() function used everywhere
- [ ] Complete ANSI escape sequence handling including OSC
- [ ] Consistent border color indexing throughout
- [ ] All changes have regression tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.723527312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:01.232066002Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:01.231998907Z","close_reason":"All children complete: bd-10h6 (visible_width consolidation, CLOSED) and bd-awkz (OSC sequence fix, CLOSED by WildSnow). lipgloss code consolidation and ANSI parsing hardening is done.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ansi-parsing","code-quality","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw3","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.747238416Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nym","title":"Fix wish e2e tests: require multi_thread tokio runtime","description":"The wish e2e tests are failing with 'can call blocking only when running on the multi-threaded runtime'. The lib.rs uses tokio::task::block_in_place which requires flavor=multi_thread, but tests use #[tokio::test] which defaults to current_thread. Fix by changing all #[tokio::test] to #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")] in the wish e2e test files.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:28:17.908864434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:29:50.398369543Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:29:50.398308128Z","close_reason":"Fixed all 13 #[tokio::test] attributes in wish e2e tests to use flavor=multi_thread for block_in_place compatibility. All 9 tests now pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nym","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:28:17.923001517Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- The showcase leans heavily on visuals; theme switching bugs are obvious and embarrassing.
- Theme behavior must be deterministic for snapshot/E2E artifacts.

Coverage:
- Preset switching:
  - switching themes updates semantic tokens (primary/success/warn/error/muted, etc.)
  - key shared helpers (`card`, `panel`, `badge`, `table_style`, etc.) produce different output as expected
- No-color / ASCII mode:
  - output contains no ANSI escapes
  - layout still uses spacing/borders appropriately
- Contrast checks (if implemented):
  - contrast validation rejects clearly invalid combinations OR emits a warning and falls back

Acceptance:
- Theme changes never leave mixed styles (no partially-updated output).
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a TTY.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireSparrow","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:07.857613637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:22:21.579547885Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oku","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:07.873808155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2oku","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:21.604530629Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2oku","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:24.186443099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2oku","depends_on_id":"bd-k52c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:22.895595502Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2pex","title":"bubbletea: Command Execution & Error Handling Improvements","description":"# Epic: bubbletea Command Execution & Error Handling

## Background
The bubbletea crate implements an Elm-architecture TUI framework. The deep review
identified several issues in the command execution and error handling subsystems.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Unbounded Thread Spawning for Batch Commands - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 834-841 in program.rs
```rust
for cmd in batch.0 {
    let tx_clone = tx.clone();
    thread::spawn(move || { ... });  // One thread per command!
}
```
**Problem**: Batch commands create one OS thread per command with no pooling or limits.
A batch of 1000 commands spawns 1000 threads.
**Impact**: Potential thread exhaustion, high OS overhead.

### 2. Silent Channel Send Failures - MEDIUM  
**Location**: Lines 610, 628, 680, 682, 687, 690, 838, 847, 852, etc.
```rust
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);  // Errors silently ignored
```
**Problem**: When main thread panics/exits, spawned command threads continue
but their messages go nowhere. No logging or visibility.
**Impact**: Silent failure, commands execute but results are lost.

### 3. Async Batch Commands Not Tracked - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 1293-1303
```rust
tokio::spawn(async move { ... });  // NOT tracked by TaskTracker
```
**Problem**: Batch commands in async context use bare tokio::spawn() instead
of tracker.spawn(). They don't receive cancellation signals during shutdown.
**Impact**: Long-running batch operations may delay shutdown.

### 4. Sequence Commands Block Async Loop - LOW
**Location**: Lines 1305-1312
**Problem**: Sequence commands execute synchronously in async context,
blocking the event loop until all complete.
**Impact**: UI freezing during sequential operations. (Documented behavior)

## Design Philosophy
- Thread pools for bounded resource usage
- Graceful degradation on channel disconnect
- Proper task tracking for clean shutdown
- Clear separation of sync vs async paths

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Batch commands use thread pool with configurable size
- [ ] Channel disconnection logged at debug level
- [ ] Async batch commands properly tracked
- [ ] All changes maintain backward compatibility","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:03.646169864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:50.331752622Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:50.331670439Z","close_reason":"All children complete: bd-3d69 (debug logging), bd-wbyt (TaskTracker for async batch), bd-3ut7 (thread pool for sync batch). Bubbletea command execution and error handling is now hardened with: bounded batch parallelism via rayon thread pool, debug logging for all channel disconnections, and proper TaskTracker integration for async batch commands.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pex","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:03.669165347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2pzh","title":"demo_showcase: pages + workflows","description":"Implement the major product surfaces of `demo_showcase` as *real application pages* (not a component gallery), and wire them together with realistic workflows.

Why:
- The showcase only “proves the stack” if the user can do meaningful, multi-step things: inspect a system, change settings, trigger work, and observe outcomes.
- Pages force integration: bubbletea routing, bubbles components, lipgloss layout/theming, glamour rendering, harmonica motion, charmed_log observability.

Design goals:
- Cohesive product story: this should feel like a premium ops console / developer tool.
- Each page is internally coherent (a workflow), and also participates in cross-page flows (e.g., a wizard creates jobs; jobs link to logs; docs explain the current screen).
- Strong information architecture: fast navigation, clear “where am I”, consistent commands.

Target pages (can evolve, but must stay comprehensive):
- Dashboard: “wow” overview, live metrics, animated deltas, drill-down.
- Jobs/Tasks: realistic queue with progress, filtering, actions, details pane.
- Logs: live tail + filtering/search + export/copy.
- Docs: in-app knowledge base rendered by glamour + search.
- Wizard/Forms: a guided flow (huh) that triggers simulated actions.
- Files: filepicker + preview (text/markdown) and common UX (errors, permissions).
- Settings: runtime toggles + theme switching + diagnostics.

Cross-page workflows (minimum):
- “Create/Deploy” (Wizard) -> creates/updates Jobs -> Jobs details links to Logs timeline.
- Docs contains a “what is this screen?” entry per page, and deep links back to pages.
- Settings changes (theme/animations/mouse/no-color/etc.) take effect immediately and are reflected across all pages.

Implementation constraints:
- Keep a single app shell (header/sidebar/main/footer) and swap page bodies via routing.
- All pages must respect:
  - Config toggles (`--no-alt-screen`, `--no-color`, `--no-animations`, etc.).
  - Resize responsiveness (reflow layout) and consistent keybindings.
  - Deterministic demo data mode for tests/E2E.

Acceptance:
- A first-time user can explore all pages without reading code and immediately “gets it”.
- Navigation feels consistent (back, page switch, help overlay) and never strands the user.
- Every page uses multiple crates in an integrated way.

Testing:
- Unit tests per page for:
  - update logic (message handling, filtering, selection stability)
  - view invariants (no panics, stable layout given fixed terminal size)
- E2E scenarios cover:
  - smoke tour across every page
  - wizard -> jobs -> logs correlation
  - docs render + search + resize reflow
  - file picker navigation + preview
  - settings toggles + theme switching
  - logs copy/export actions

Notes:
- Avoid “toy dashboards” with random numbers; prefer a deterministic simulation that looks plausible.
- Favor usability over maximal density: always keep a clear primary path + shortcuts for power users.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:08.023630690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:08.434450914Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pages"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pzh","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:08.039286770Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -147,8 +147,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-2xlv","title":"demo_showcase theme: load custom theme from file + contrast checks","description":"Optional but compelling showcase: load a custom theme from disk and validate it.

Why:
- It proves the theming system isn’t hard-coded and can be user-configured.
- Contrast validation demonstrates a mature design approach (readability matters).

Requirements:
- CLI/Config:
  - support `--theme-file <path>` (takes precedence over preset theme)
- File format:
  - pick a single stable format (recommend: TOML or JSON) and document the schema
  - map file values into semantic tokens (not raw ad-hoc styling)
- Validation:
  - validate required fields are present
  - run contrast checks for key pairs (fg/bg, muted/bg, selection/bg)
  - surface warnings in About/Diagnostics and/or as a toast on load
- Failure handling:
  - invalid file should not crash; fall back to a safe preset and show a clear error

Testability:
- Add unit tests for:
  - parsing valid theme files
  - rejecting invalid/missing fields
  - contrast warning behavior

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates lipgloss theme IO + validation in a real workflow.
- The app remains readable and stable even with bad user themes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.588370671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:46.886871699Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:43.964560626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.601173399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:40.699647818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xzk","title":"huh: Form Component Bug Fixes & Robustness","description":"# Epic: huh Form Component Bug Fixes & Robustness

## Background
The huh crate provides interactive form components (Select, MultiSelect, Text, FilePicker).
Deep review identified several bugs and edge cases that affect reliability.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Select Field Missing Empty Guard - FIXED ✓
**Location**: Line 1864 (was missing guard, now fixed)
**Status**: Fixed in this review session

### 2. MultiSelect Cursor Position with Filtering - HIGH
**Location**: Lines 2231, 2185-2198
**Problem**: When filtering is applied, cursor position calculation uses wrong index space.
`self.cursor` indexes unfiltered list but calculation assumes filtered list indices.
**Impact**: Cursor appears at wrong position after filtering.

### 3. Select Field Incomplete Filter Support - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 1610, 1815-1875
**Problem**: Select has filter_value field and calls filtered_options(), but navigation
logic doesn't respect filtered state. Navigation may jump to non-visible items.
**Impact**: Poor UX when filtering - navigation broken.

### 4. FilePicker Single-File Edge Case - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 3660-3671
**Problem**: With single file, navigation condition prevents selection.
`if self.selected_index < self.files.len().saturating_sub(1)` is always false
when len=1 (0 < 0 is false).
**Impact**: Single-file pickers non-functional.

### 5. Form Initialization Repeated - LOW
**Location**: Lines 4621-4628
**Problem**: init_fields() called on every update while state is Normal and
current_group is 0. Could cause repeated initialization.
**Impact**: Minor performance, potential state corruption.

## Design Philosophy
- All edge cases should be explicitly handled
- Empty/single-item states should work correctly
- Filter state should be fully integrated with navigation
- Defensive programming throughout

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All identified bugs fixed with tests
- [ ] Edge cases for 0, 1, and many items all work
- [ ] Filtering properly integrated with navigation
- [ ] No regression in existing functionality","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:19:12.348259552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:09.230039458Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:09.229971211Z","close_reason":"All children complete: bd-17p2 (MultiSelect cursor, CLOSED), bd-8wrz (Select filter, CLOSED by WildSnow), bd-1isw (FilePicker single-file, CLOSED by GoldPeak). huh form component bug fixes and robustness hardening is done.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug-fixes","forms","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xzk","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:19:12.365634836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zeq","title":"demo_showcase: quality gates (tests/docs/perf)","description":"Ensure demo_showcase is reliable and maintainable.

Acceptance:

-{"id":"bd-2zj3","title":"E2E Tests: demo_showcase navigation flows","description":"End-to-end tests for demo_showcase navigation and page flows.

## Purpose
Test complete user workflows through demo_showcase,
verifying the app works as a real user would experience it.

## Test Requirements

### Application Startup
- [ ] App starts and renders initial dashboard
- [ ] Sidebar shows all pages
- [ ] Status bar displays correctly
- [ ] Help overlay accessible via '?'

### Page Navigation
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts 1-8 navigate pages
- [ ] Sidebar selection works
- [ ] Page state persists during navigation
- [ ] Back navigation if implemented

### Each Page Smoke Test
- [ ] Dashboard - metrics load and display
- [ ] Services - service list renders
- [ ] Jobs - job list with progress
- [ ] Logs - log entries display, filter works
- [ ] Docs - documentation tree navigable
- [ ] Files - file browser works
- [ ] Wizard - can start wizard flow
- [ ] Settings - settings toggle correctly

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log every key press with timestamp
- [ ] Log expected vs actual page after nav
- [ ] Capture terminal state on failure
- [ ] Include timing information

## Success Criteria
- All navigation paths tested
- Each page renders without error
- No dead-end states","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:27.855435034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:38.911223602Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:27.878940499Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:03.377359642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:38.911195389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2zkx","title":"Integration Tests: Cross-platform terminal support","description":"Integration tests for cross-platform terminal compatibility.

## Purpose
Ensure charmed_rust works correctly across different
terminals and platforms.

## Test Requirements

### Terminal Capability Detection
- [ ] Color support detection (16/256/RGB)
- [ ] Unicode support detection
- [ ] Mouse support detection
- [ ] Alternate screen support

### Graceful Degradation
- [ ] ASCII-only fallback mode
- [ ] 16-color fallback
- [ ] No-mouse fallback
- [ ] Dumb terminal handling

### Platform-Specific
- [ ] Linux terminal emulators
- [ ] macOS Terminal.app
- [ ] Windows Terminal
- [ ] Windows ConHost (legacy)
- [ ] SSH sessions
- [ ] tmux/screen

### Environment Variables
- [ ] TERM handling
- [ ] COLORTERM handling
- [ ] NO_COLOR support
- [ ] FORCE_COLOR support
- [ ] CI detection

## Test Approach
Use environment variable mocking to simulate
different terminal environments.

## Success Criteria
- Works in all major terminals
- Degrades gracefully when features unavailable
- Respects user terminal preferences","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldPeak","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:14.596526172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:16:07.915594808Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-platform","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zkx","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:14.612515273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2zj3","title":"E2E Tests: demo_showcase navigation flows","description":"End-to-end tests for demo_showcase navigation and page flows.

## Purpose
Test complete user workflows through demo_showcase,
verifying the app works as a real user would experience it.

## Test Requirements

### Application Startup
- [ ] App starts and renders initial dashboard
- [ ] Sidebar shows all pages
- [ ] Status bar displays correctly
- [ ] Help overlay accessible via '?'

### Page Navigation
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts 1-8 navigate pages
- [ ] Sidebar selection works
- [ ] Page state persists during navigation
- [ ] Back navigation if implemented

### Each Page Smoke Test
- [ ] Dashboard - metrics load and display
- [ ] Services - service list renders
- [ ] Jobs - job list with progress
- [ ] Logs - log entries display, filter works
- [ ] Docs - documentation tree navigable
- [ ] Files - file browser works
- [ ] Wizard - can start wizard flow
- [ ] Settings - settings toggle correctly

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log every key press with timestamp
- [ ] Log expected vs actual page after nav
- [ ] Capture terminal state on failure
- [ ] Include timing information

## Success Criteria
- All navigation paths tested
- Each page renders without error
- No dead-end states","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldPeak","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:27.855435034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:32:43.682530345Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:27.878940499Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:03.377359642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:38.911195389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2zkx","title":"Integration Tests: Cross-platform terminal support","description":"Integration tests for cross-platform terminal compatibility.

## Purpose
Ensure charmed_rust works correctly across different
terminals and platforms.

## Test Requirements

### Terminal Capability Detection
- [ ] Color support detection (16/256/RGB)
- [ ] Unicode support detection
- [ ] Mouse support detection
- [ ] Alternate screen support

### Graceful Degradation
- [ ] ASCII-only fallback mode
- [ ] 16-color fallback
- [ ] No-mouse fallback
- [ ] Dumb terminal handling

### Platform-Specific
- [ ] Linux terminal emulators
- [ ] macOS Terminal.app
- [ ] Windows Terminal
- [ ] Windows ConHost (legacy)
- [ ] SSH sessions
- [ ] tmux/screen

### Environment Variables
- [ ] TERM handling
- [ ] COLORTERM handling
- [ ] NO_COLOR support
- [ ] FORCE_COLOR support
- [ ] CI detection

## Test Approach
Use environment variable mocking to simulate
different terminal environments.

## Success Criteria
- Works in all major terminals
- Degrades gracefully when features unavailable
- Respects user terminal preferences","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldPeak","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:14.596526172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:57.615013775Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:57.614885346Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-platform","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zkx","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:14.612515273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-30md","title":"Animations: page transitions + focus highlight","description":"Add high-impact motion polish: page transitions + focus highlight.

Why:
- Smooth transitions are a huge part of the "turned up to 11" feel.
- This also demonstrates harmonica-based animation integrated into bubbletea’s update loop.

What to animate:
- Page transition:
  - subtle slide/fade or wipe between pages (Dashboard → Jobs → Docs, etc.)
  - transition should be fast and never feel sluggish
- Focus highlight:
  - clearly indicate which pane is focused (sidebar vs main vs modal)
  - when focus changes, animate the highlight rather than snapping (unless reduce-motion)

Constraints:
- Must respect `--no-animations` / reduce-motion:
  - animations become instant state changes
- Must never cause layout corruption:
  - transitions should not change measured widths/heights mid-frame

Testability:
- Animation invariants are covered by the animation unit tests.
- Key UI snapshots should include at least one focused vs unfocused view.

Acceptance:
- Transitions feel premium and consistent.
- Reduce-motion mode still looks good (no broken visuals).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:56.791266325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:35.043791745Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","polish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:56.825831852Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:11.824345470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:10.604501900Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32ba","title":"SSH mode: run the showcase UI per SSH session","description":"For each SSH session, run the bubbletea program with the session stdin/stdout.

Implementation:
- use bubbletea custom I/O (`with_input`, `with_output`) so the runtime doesn’t touch local terminal state.
- ensure window size propagation from SSH PTY.

Acceptance:
- The same UI (or a curated subset) is usable over SSH.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:51.571123791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:28.647204587Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","wish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ba","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:39.056411877Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32ba","depends_on_id":"bd-3chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:51.587418450Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32ba","depends_on_id":"bd-3xs0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:50.645475913Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32ba","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:53.724307436Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32pp","title":"Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log","description":"Implement the *styled* log formatting layer for the Logs page using `charmed_log`.

Why:
- A flagship ops-style demo lives and dies by log readability.
- This showcases that `charmed_log` + lipgloss styling can produce beautiful, information-dense output.

Formatting requirements:
- Each log entry should include (where available):
  - timestamp (compact)
  - level (TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
  - service/component name
  - message
- Styling:
  - color-coded levels (warn/error pop, info muted)
  - consistent column alignment and truncation
  - preserve legibility in narrow terminals
- No-color mode:
  - degrade gracefully (no ANSI), still aligned and readable

Integration:
- Logs are produced by the demo domain simulation and by user actions.
- The formatted line should carry the structured fields needed for filtering (level/service/query) without re-parsing the rendered string.

Acceptance:
- Logs look great, are readable at a glance, and remain usable with thousands of lines.
- Deterministic formatting (important for snapshot/E2E artifacts).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:37.574456901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:16.801798652Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:16.801717932Z","close_reason":"Implemented LogFormatter with styled, aligned output supporting color-coded levels, no-color mode, and configurable column widths. Added 8 tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:37.594269536Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-33fe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:28.585526827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:31.153270873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3mh2","title":"demo_showcase theme: semantic tokens + shared style helpers","description":"Build the styling foundation for the entire app.

Implement:
- A theme module that maps lipgloss `ThemeContext` (ColorSlots) into semantic UI primitives.
- Shared helpers: `card()`, `panel()`, `badge()`, `button()`, `table_style()`, `modal()`, `status_bar()`, etc.

Guidelines:
- No ad-hoc hex strings scattered across pages.
- Prefer semantic roles (primary/success/warn/error/muted) and allow theme switching.

Acceptance:
- A page can be built using only shared style helpers + minimal page-specific overrides.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:23.252512028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:37:33.002106933Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:37:33.002041060Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-2ucj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:32.937693109Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:23.270859361Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mh2","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:31.724599289Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mtt","title":"demo_showcase core: command palette (search + actions)","description":"Optional but very compelling polish feature: a command palette ("quick actions") that makes the app feel modern.

Why:
- It dramatically improves discoverability and speed for power users.
- It showcases composing bubbles components (textinput + list) into a cohesive UX.

UX:
- Trigger:
  - `/` (or another agreed key) opens the palette
  - `esc` closes
- Layout:
  - text input at top
  - filtered list of commands below
- Behavior:
  - fuzzy-ish matching over command names + keywords
  - enter runs selected action
  - shows short descriptions and shortcuts (optional)

Actions to include (minimum):
- Navigation:
  - jump to Dashboard/Jobs/Docs/Wizard/Files/Logs/Settings
- Toggles:
  - toggle theme picker / cycle theme
  - toggle no-color / reduce-motion
- Utilities:
  - export current view (plain/HTML)
  - open diagnostics (pager / export)

Implementation guidance:
- Maintain a small registry of commands (id, title, keywords, handler).
- Handlers should emit bubbletea Cmds / messages (no direct side effects in UI code).

Acceptance:
- Palette is keyboard-friendly, fast, and feels integrated (not a bolted-on demo).
- Actions are safe in headless/self-check mode (no hangs).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.480166189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:37.827452925Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:26.750440223Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:28.035096998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:02.499012063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mxk","title":"Jobs: progress visualization polish","description":"Polish job progress visualization so it looks like a modern CI dashboard (compelling, readable, information-dense).

Why:
- Progress UI is where “toy” demos instantly reveal themselves.
- This task should make the Jobs page feel premium even in a plain terminal.

Requirements:
- Use `bubbles::progress` for determinate progress:
  - clear fill, percentage (optional), and label
  - themed colors that match status (running/success/fail/cancel)
- Use spinners for indeterminate states:
  - e.g., “waiting”, “queued”, “uploading”, “finalizing”
- Status clarity:
  - icons/chips for state
  - duration + ETA-ish hint when possible
  - differentiate “stuck” vs “slow” (e.g., warn color after threshold)
- Visual polish:
  - consistent spacing and alignment across rows
  - good behavior in narrow widths (truncate/wrap rules)
  - respects `--no-color` (still readable) and `--no-animations`

Optional (nice if it’s easy):
- Stage-based progress (build/test/deploy) with per-stage completion markers.

Acceptance:
- Looks like a real CI dashboard.
- Clear at a glance: what’s running, what’s blocked, what failed, what finished.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - rendering decisions given job state (spinner vs bar, colors/labels)
  - width-constrained rendering produces stable output
- E2E:
  - wizard -> jobs scenario should assert at least one running job shows an updating progress indicator.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.217085325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:43:57.767439291Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","progress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:23.232965048Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:56.844461854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mxk","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:59.582343606Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- [ ] Invalid characters rejected

- [ ] Logs generated in Logs page
- [ ] Dashboard metrics update

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log each wizard step transition
- [ ] Log validation attempts/results
- [ ] Log deployment progress events
- [ ] Full form state at submission

## Success Criteria
- Complete wizard flow works E2E
- Validation prevents bad input
- Deployment integrates across pages","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:41.429691632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:40.423607800Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:41.456013478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:05.929237471Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nit","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:40.423566914Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3nyf","title":"EPIC: Integration Test Coverage","description":"Integration tests that verify cross-crate behavior and real terminal interaction.

## Philosophy
- Test how crates work together
- Test actual terminal I/O where possible
- Verify ANSI escape sequence correctness
- Test across different terminal capabilities

## Key Integration Points
1. bubbletea + bubbles - components in event loop
2. bubbletea + lipgloss - styled output rendering
3. glamour + lipgloss - markdown with styles
4. huh + bubbletea - form in TUI context
5. charmed_log + any - logging integration

## Test Infrastructure Needs
- Terminal emulator/PTY test harness
- ANSI sequence capture and verification
- Cross-platform terminal capability detection
- Timing-sensitive test handling","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.653513760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.663266853Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nyf","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:41.663223292Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3o6n","title":"QA and Stabilization Session: wish, lipgloss, glamour, huh","description":"Completed a deep dive review and fix cycle for the codebase.

Fixed:
- wish: Output flushing, bubbletea integration, input handling.
- lipgloss: Theme initialization bugs, missing error types.
- glamour: Table rendering (replaced naive implementation with table.rs), updated tests.
- huh: Doc examples, missing helper methods for value extraction.
- charmed_log: Date math (migrated to chrono).

Verified:
- All crates compile cleanly.
- Tests pass for all modified crates.
- Workspace check passes.

Closed related epics/tasks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:22.301048189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525183754Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525106669Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
